summary: Iteration 58 - Driver PIN Authentication Testing COMPLETE. Backend: 9/10 tests passed (1 skipped). Frontend: Driver App PIN login working. Found BUG: Custom PIN not saved when creating driver via JSON body.

critical.issue: Custom PIN not saved when creating driver via JSON body. Driver is created with default PIN '1234' instead of the provided PIN.
critical.root_cause: Backend endpoint uses query parameters but frontend sends JSON body. FastAPI interprets the body using DriverCreate model which has default pin='1234'. The custom PIN from the body is ignored.
critical.reproduction: 1. Create driver with JSON body {name, phone, pin: '5678', branch_id}. 2. Try login with PIN '5678' - fails. 3. Try login with PIN '1234' - succeeds.
critical.fix_suggestion: Modify create_driver endpoint to accept DriverCreate model as body parameter instead of individual query params

minor.issue: Same issue - endpoint expects query params but frontend sends JSON body. PIN update may not work correctly.

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/driver/login with correct PIN - PASSED (returns driver info)
Backend: POST /api/driver/login with wrong PIN - PASSED (returns 401)
Backend: POST /api/driver/login without PIN - PASSED (returns 422 validation error)
Backend: POST /api/drivers creates driver - PASSED (but PIN bug documented)
Backend: Driver login with inactive driver - PASSED (returns 401 due to PIN check order)
Backend: Special characters in PIN - PASSED (returns 401)
Frontend: Driver App login page has PIN field - PASSED
Frontend: Driver App login with correct PIN - PASSED
Frontend: Driver App login with wrong PIN shows error - PASSED
Frontend: Admin panel Add Driver dialog has PIN field - PASSED
Frontend: Admin panel Edit Driver dialog has PIN field - PASSED
skipped_tests: test_05_update_driver_pin - Skipped due to PIN update endpoint issues
action_items: FIX CRITICAL: Modify POST /api/drivers to accept DriverCreate model as body parameter
FIX: Modify PUT /api/drivers/{driver_id} to accept body parameters for updates
VERIFY: After fix, test that custom PIN is saved correctly when creating driver
critical_code_review_comments: BUG: Backend endpoint POST /api/drivers uses query params (name: str, phone: str, pin: str = '1234') but frontend sends JSON body. FastAPI uses DriverCreate model for body parsing which has default pin='1234', ignoring the custom PIN.
The driver login API (POST /api/driver/login) works correctly with phone and PIN parameters
PIN is correctly NOT returned in API responses for security
Driver App UI correctly shows PIN field and validates input
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_driver_pin_auth_iter58.py - Created comprehensive PIN auth test file
context_for_next_testing_agent: Driver PIN authentication is implemented but has a BUG: when creating a driver via JSON body, the custom PIN is not saved - driver gets default PIN '1234'. The driver login API (POST /api/driver/login?phone=X&pin=Y) works correctly. Frontend UI has PIN fields in both Add Driver and Edit Driver dialogs. Test driver exists: phone=07901234567, pin=1234.

driver_login_with_pin.notes: Returns driver info on success, 401 on wrong PIN, 404 on unknown phone

driver_app_ui_pin_field.notes: Has phone and PIN input fields, shows appropriate error messages

admin_panel_pin_field.notes: Add Driver and Edit Driver dialogs have PIN fields

create_driver_with_pin.notes: Custom PIN ignored, driver created with default PIN '1234'

update_driver_pin.notes: May have same issue as create endpoint

bug_details.title: Custom PIN not saved when creating driver
reproduction_steps: 4. Fill name, phone, and custom PIN (e.g., '5678')
6. Try to login as driver with the custom PIN - FAILS
7. Try to login with default PIN '1234' - SUCCEEDS
bug_details.expected_behavior: Driver should be created with the custom PIN provided
bug_details.actual_behavior: Driver is created with default PIN '1234' regardless of input
bug_details.root_cause: Backend endpoint signature uses query parameters but frontend sends JSON body. FastAPI uses DriverCreate Pydantic model for body parsing which has default pin='1234'
